name: OpenAI Agents SDK — sessions, trimming, and memory notes
context_primitives: OpenAI documents **context engineering** explicitly: **Session** objects auto-persist history; cookbook recommends **trimming** (keep last N turns—deterministic, no summarizer variance) vs **compression** (summarize older turns). Separate cookbook on **long-term memory notes** for personalization. Responses/Conversations APIs for server-persisted threads. Philosophy: **curate** history before stuffing GPT-5-class windows (272k input cited in cookbook).
deployment_pattern: Agents SDK Session + RunConfig; apply trimming policies for tool-heavy loops; use Conversations API for cross-device continuity; optional memory notes layer for prefs.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Cookbook emphasizes coherence and cost, not LOCOMO scores; aligns with production eval mindset in DeLucia talk.
ecosystem_integrations: Codex, ChatGPT agents, third-party hosts on OpenAI API.
differentiators: vs. **Alex**: OpenAI **endorses trimming as first-class** (similar spirit to head/tail keeping recent verbatim) and treats **summarization/compression as optional**—matches DeLucia's **distrust of uncontrolled summarization** but OpenAI still documents compression as a technique. Alex adds **explicit external store + tool recall by ID** rather than only dropping old turns.
significance_for_builders: Official guidance supports **deterministic context policies + evals** over hope-and-pray full context; combine SDK trimming with **external memory** (Mem0/DB) for cross-session facts Alex still lacks.
key_timeline_or_milestones: 2025 OpenAI cookbook 'Context Engineering - Short-Term Memory Management with Sessions'; Agents SDK session docs.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI Cookbook — session memory / context engineering
